behaviors.description: Rows with missing required fields are rejected and appear in the dead-letter queue with the original row number.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at module handoff (CSV parser row index -> validator -> DLQ writer), not just one function. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess is required to reproduce this; in-process wiring is enough. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text only requires 'appear in the dead-letter queue with the original row number' and does not require real S3/queue/network semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Run the pipeline in-process on a fixture CSV with missing required fields; use real parser+validator+DLQ adapter wiring (test double storage is fine). Assert each invalid row is rejected, recorded in DLQ, and includes the original CSV row number.

behaviors.description: The transformation correctly converts date strings in multiple formats (ISO 8601, US, European) to a single canonical format.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this behavior is pure transformation logic; failure is within one function/object boundary. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text is strictly 'transformation correctly converts date strings... to a single canonical format,' which is deterministic parsing/formatting logic.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven unit tests over representative ISO 8601, US, and European date strings (plus edge cases like ambiguous day/month) asserting exact canonical output or explicit parse failure.

behaviors.description: A CSV file with 500,000 rows completes processing within the configured timeout without running out of memory.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: timeout and memory-exhaustion failures are process/runtime behaviors that mocks hide; this needs real runtime characteristics. MOCK-HIDE: in-process stubs for timing/memory can pass while real execution OOMs or times out. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly requires 'within the configured timeout' and 'without running out of memory,' which depend on real execution environment.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the pipeline as a real process against a generated 500k-row CSV and real configured timeout, while measuring wall-clock completion and peak memory usage; fail if timeout is exceeded or memory crosses the defined budget/OOM occurs.

behaviors.description: When the S3 bucket returns a transient 503 error, the pipeline retries with exponential backoff rather than failing the entire batch.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: failure boundary is HTTP/service-error behavior ('S3 bucket returns a transient 503'). MOCK-HIDE: mocked clients often bypass SDK/HTTP error translation and retry timing behavior, hiding real failures. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because the behavior text explicitly depends on real 503 service responses and backoff behavior under real request handling.
behaviors.test_description: Run pipeline against a real HTTP S3-compatible endpoint/test harness that returns 503 for initial requests then succeeds; assert retry count, exponentially increasing delays (within tolerance), and that batch processing completes instead of aborting.

behaviors.description: Rows successfully written to PostgreSQL are not duplicated if the pipeline is restarted mid-batch.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS/REAL-INFRA: this is crash/restart recovery with persistence boundaries (process restart + DB state). Mocking restart or DB semantics can hide duplicate-write bugs. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly requires correctness 'if the pipeline is restarted mid-batch,' which is a real process lifecycle boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Use real PostgreSQL and run the pipeline as a subprocess; force stop mid-batch after partial commits, restart with same input, then assert final table has no duplicates (via unique key/idempotency checks) and row count equals expected unique rows.
